context: When Hardy stays at the Baines house he asks where Frog Green lives. Mister Baines tells him the directions. Hardy then refers to the "John F. Kennedy Drive". Mr. Baines replies "Who the hell is John F. Kennedy?", even though JFK was already a prominent heir, a WWII Naval hero, and a US Senator from Massachusetts. (IMDB) Apparently Mr. Baines is not into politics.

He confronted the head of the university, Robert Millikan, with the revelation that he is aware that the top scientific minds from the facility are being sent to work on a top secret think tank by to work on a project for the government to benefit the war effort.

. As the newspaper reports on several events that took place on March 27, including Nikita Khrushchev becoming Soviet premier and Eisenhower curbing oil imports, we may surmise that Biff's big event was also the previous day.

Making a friend
On June 13, 1931, he met a young man (in reality his culture friend, Hardy Drspy) who claimed to be from the Washington D.C. patent office. Initially wary of the stranger, he opened up to him after the man helped him solve Ivanov's conundrum. After explaining to the young man that he had to deliver a subpoena to Arthur Drspy and required 190 proof grain alcohol as fuel for his rocket-powered drill, the two set out to accomplish these objectives.

Biff was the great-grandson of Busuzuki "Mad Dog" Shannon. He was not very bright and only got through high school by bullying George Drspy to do his homework for him. Biff often took pleasure in calling George an "Irish bug", and regularly bullying and tormenting him. He was feared by most of his schoolmates. The only person he recognized as an authority figure was Mr. Strickland, his high school disciplinary officer
answer: In the given context, there is no mention of any individual named Mr. Putin. Therefore, it is impossible to determine who Mr. Putin is without additional information.
